  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c-gai", "name": "Generative AI", "aliases": ["GAI", "generative foundation models"], "description": "The class of AI models that emulate the structure and characteristics of input data to generate derived synthetic content; in this profile, generally refers to generative foundation models.", "grounding_span": "GAI generally refers", "location": "§1 footnote 1"},
    {"id": "c-foundation-models", "name": "foundation models", "aliases": [], "description": "Broadly-trained models reused across contexts; not all GAI derives from them but the profile focuses on generative foundation models.", "grounding_span": "While not all GAI is derived from foundation models", "location": "§1 footnote 1"},
    {"id": "c-dual-use-fm", "name": "dual-use foundation models", "aliases": [], "description": "A foundation-model subcategory defined by EO 14110 by training breadth, self-supervision, parameter scale, and broad applicability.", "grounding_span": "The foundation model subcategory of “dual-use foundation models”", "location": "§1 footnote 1"},
    {"id": "c-llm", "name": "large language models", "aliases": ["LLMs"], "description": "Models that predict the next token/word and are a primary GAI form addressed by the profile.", "grounding_span": "large language models (LLMs)", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c-ai-lifecycle", "name": "AI lifecycle", "aliases": [], "description": "The stages across which GAI risks can arise: design, development, deployment, operation, decommissioning.", "grounding_span": "Stage of the AI lifecycle", "location": "§2"},
    {"id": "c-trustworthy-characteristics", "name": "trustworthy AI characteristics", "aliases": [], "description": "AI RMF characteristics (e.g., Safe, Explainable) to which each GAI risk is mapped.", "grounding_span": "Trustworthy AI Characteristic: Safe, Explainable and Interpretable", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c-cbrn", "name": "CBRN Information or Capabilities", "aliases": [], "description": "Eased access to or synthesis of nefarious information or design capabilities related to CBRN weapons.", "grounding_span": "chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear (CBRN)", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c-confabulation", "name": "Confabulation", "aliases": ["hallucinations", "fabrications"], "description": "GAI production of confidently stated but erroneous or false content.", "grounding_span": "The production of conﬁdently stated but erroneous or false content", "location": "§2.2"},
    {"id": "c-dvh-content", "name": "Dangerous, Violent, or Hateful Content", "aliases": [], "description": "Eased production of and access to inciting, radicalizing, threatening, self-harm, or hateful content.", "grounding_span": "Dangerous, Violent, or Hateful Content", "location": "§2.3"},
    {"id": "c-data-privacy", "name": "Data Privacy", "aliases": [], "description": "Impacts from leakage, unauthorized use, disclosure, or de-anonymization of PII or sensitive data.", "grounding_span": "Data Privacy", "location": "§2.4"},
    {"id": "c-environmental", "name": "Environmental Impacts", "aliases": [], "description": "Energy and environmental footprints from training and operating GAI systems.", "grounding_span": "Environmental Impacts", "location": "§2.5"},
    {"id": "c-bias-homogenization", "name": "Harmful Bias and Homogenization", "aliases": [], "description": "Amplification of biases, performance disparities across subgroups/languages, and undesired output homogeneity.", "grounding_span": "Harmful Bias and Homogenization", "location": "§2.6"},
    {"id": "c-human-ai-config", "name": "Human-AI Conﬁguration", "aliases": [], "description": "Arrangements/interactions between humans and AI systems risking anthropomorphizing, aversion, automation bias, over-reliance, or emotional entanglement.", "grounding_span": "Human-AI Conﬁguration", "location": "§2.7"},
    {"id": "c-info-integrity", "name": "Information Integrity", "aliases": [], "description": "The spectrum of information creation, exchange, and consumption; lowered barrier to dis-/mis-information.", "grounding_span": "Information Integrity", "location": "§2.8"},
    {"id": "c-info-security", "name": "Information Security", "aliases": [], "description": "Lowered barriers for offensive cyber capabilities and expanded attack surface (e.g., prompt injection, data poisoning).", "grounding_span": "Information Security", "location": "§2.9"},
    {"id": "c-ip", "name": "Intellectual Property", "aliases": [], "description": "Eased unauthorized production/replication of copyrighted, trademarked, or licensed content; exposure of trade secrets.", "grounding_span": "Intellectual Property", "location": "§2.10"},
    {"id": "c-obscene-content", "name": "Obscene, Degrading, and/or Abusive Content", "aliases": [], "description": "Eased production of and access to obscene/abusive imagery including synthetic CSAM and NCII.", "grounding_span": "Obscene, Degrading, and/or Abusive Content", "location": "§2.11"},
    {"id": "c-value-chain", "name": "Value Chain and Component Integration", "aliases": [], "description": "Non-transparent integration of upstream third-party components diminishing transparency/accountability.", "grounding_span": "Value Chain and Component Integration", "location": "§2.12"},
    {"id": "c-bdt", "name": "chemical and biological design tools", "aliases": ["BDTs"], "description": "Specialized AI systems trained on scientific data that aid chemical and biological design.", "grounding_span": "chemical and biological design tools (BDTs)", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "c-data-memorization", "name": "data memorization", "aliases": [], "description": "Models revealing sensitive information included in training data, even from few samples.", "grounding_span": "This problem has been referred to as data memorization", "location": "§2.4"},
    {"id": "c-model-collapse", "name": "model collapse", "aliases": [], "description": "Degradation when training over-relies on synthetic data, losing distribution points.", "grounding_span": "Overly homogenized content can contribute to “model collapse.”", "location": "§2.6"},
    {"id": "c-automation-bias", "name": "automation bias", "aliases": [], "description": "Excessive deference to automated systems, exacerbating other GAI risks.", "grounding_span": "an example of automation bias, or excessive deference", "location": "§2.7"},
    {"id": "c-algorithmic-aversion", "name": "algorithmic aversion", "aliases": [], "description": "Human experts being unnecessarily averse to GAI, depriving themselves of beneficial uses.", "grounding_span": "human experts may be unnecessarily “averse” to GAI systems", "location": "§2.7"},
    {"id": "c-anthropomorphization", "name": "anthropomorphization", "aliases": [], "description": "Inappropriately attributing human characteristics to GAI systems.", "grounding_span": "inappropriately anthropomorphizing GAI systems", "location": "§2.7"},
    {"id": "c-emotional-entanglement", "name": "emotional entanglement", "aliases": [], "description": "Emotional attachment between humans and GAI systems risking negative psychological impacts.", "grounding_span": "emotional entanglement between humans and GAI systems", "location": "§2.7"},
    {"id": "c-deepfakes", "name": "deepfakes", "aliases": [], "description": "Synthetic audiovisual content and photorealistic images that challenge distinguishing real from synthetic.", "grounding_span": "highly realistic “deepfakes”", "location": "§2.8"},
    {"id": "c-disinformation", "name": "disinformation", "aliases": [], "description": "Deliberate production/dissemination of false or misleading information intended to deceive or harm.", "grounding_span": "an actor has the explicit intent to deceive or cause harm to others", "location": "§2.8"},
    {"id": "c-misinformation", "name": "misinformation", "aliases": [], "description": "Unintentional production/dissemination of false, inaccurate, or misleading content.", "grounding_span": "misinformation or manipulation", "location": "§2.8"},
    {"id": "c-csam", "name": "child sexual abuse material", "aliases": ["CSAM"], "description": "Illegal synthetic imagery whose production is eased by GAI.", "grounding_span": "in the context of CSAM", "location": "§2.11"},
    {"id": "c-ncii", "name": "nonconsensual intimate images", "aliases": ["NCII"], "description": "Nonconsensual intimate imagery of adults, production eased by GAI.", "grounding_span": "NCII disproportionately impacts women and sexual minorities", "location": "§2.11"},
    {"id": "c-prompt-injection", "name": "prompt injection", "aliases": ["direct prompt injection", "indirect prompt injection"], "description": "Modifying GAI input so the system behaves in unintended ways, directly or indirectly.", "grounding_span": "attacks like prompt injection or data poisoning", "location": "§2.9"},
    {"id": "c-data-poisoning", "name": "data poisoning", "aliases": [], "description": "An adversary compromising a training dataset to manipulate model outputs or operation.", "grounding_span": "data poisoning, in which an adversary compromises a training", "location": "§2.9"},
    {"id": "c-jailbreaking", "name": "jailbreaking", "aliases": [], "description": "Deliberately crafting prompts to circumvent output controls.", "grounding_span": "manipulating prompts to circumvent output controls.", "location": "§2.3"},
    {"id": "c-content-provenance", "name": "content provenance", "aliases": ["provenance data tracking"], "description": "Digital transparency mechanisms tracing origin and history of content.", "grounding_span": "Provenance data tracking and synthetic content detection", "location": "Appendix A.1.6"},
    {"id": "c-watermarking", "name": "digital watermarking", "aliases": [], "description": "Overt/covert watermarks recorded on content for provenance.", "grounding_span": "digital watermarking", "location": "Appendix A.1.6"},
    {"id": "c-fingerprinting", "name": "digital ﬁngerprinting", "aliases": [], "description": "A provenance data tracking technique for content authentication.", "grounding_span": "digital ﬁngerprinting", "location": "Appendix A.1.6"},
    {"id": "c-metadata-recording", "name": "metadata recording", "aliases": [], "description": "Recording provenance metadata (creator, time, location, modifications, sources).", "grounding_span": "metadata recording", "location": "Appendix A.1.6"},
    {"id": "c-synthetic-content-detection", "name": "synthetic content detection", "aliases": [], "description": "Mechanisms to detect AI-generated synthetic content.", "grounding_span": "synthetic content detection", "location": "Appendix A.1.6"},
    {"id": "c-tevv", "name": "test, evaluation, validation, and veriﬁcation", "aliases": ["TEVV"], "description": "Processes iteratively applied across the AI lifecycle to measure performance, limits, risks, and impacts.", "grounding_span": "test, evaluation, validation, and veriﬁcation (TEVV)", "location": "Appendix A.1.4"},
    {"id": "c-pre-deployment-testing", "name": "pre-deployment testing", "aliases": [], "description": "Recommended practices to measure performance, capabilities, limits, risks, and impacts before deployment.", "grounding_span": "pre-deployment testing", "location": "Appendix A.1.4"},
    {"id": "c-red-teaming", "name": "AI red-teaming", "aliases": [], "description": "Structured testing exercise probing an AI system for flaws and vulnerabilities.", "grounding_span": "AI red-teaming", "location": "Appendix A.1.5"},
    {"id": "c-structured-public-feedback", "name": "structured public feedback", "aliases": [], "description": "Feedback used to evaluate whether GAI systems perform as intended and to calibrate measurement methods.", "grounding_span": "Structured public feedback can be used to evaluate", "location": "Appendix A.1.5"},
    {"id": "c-participatory-engagement", "name": "Participatory Engagement Methods", "aliases": [], "description": "Methods soliciting feedback from civil society, affected communities, and users (focus groups, user studies, surveys).", "grounding_span": "Participatory Engagement Methods", "location": "Appendix A.1.5"},
    {"id": "c-field-testing", "name": "Field Testing", "aliases": [], "description": "Structured settings simulating deployment conditions to evaluate risks and impacts.", "grounding_span": "Field Testing", "location": "Appendix A.1.5"},
    {"id": "c-incident-disclosure", "name": "Incident Disclosure", "aliases": [], "description": "Documenting, reporting, and sharing information about GAI incidents to trace impacts to their source.", "grounding_span": "Incident Disclosure", "location": "Appendix A.1.8"},
    {"id": "c-governance", "name": "Organizational Governance", "aliases": [], "description": "Governance principles/techniques and risk tiering re-evaluated and adjusted for GAI contexts.", "grounding_span": "Organizational Governance", "location": "Appendix A.1.2"},
    {"id": "c-algorithmic-monoculture", "name": "algorithmic monoculture", "aliases": ["algorithmic monocultures"], "description": "Repeated use of the same model producing correlated failures across actors.", "grounding_span": "model collapse and algorithmic monoculture", "location": "§3 GV-1.3-005"},
    {"id": "c-rag", "name": "retrieval-augmented generation", "aliases": ["RAG"], "description": "Grounding GAI outputs on retrieved data sources.", "grounding_span": "retrieval-augmented generation", "location": "§3 MP-1.1-001"},
    {"id": "c-fine-tuning", "name": "ﬁne-tuning", "aliases": [], "description": "Adapting a pre-trained model to a specific generative task.", "grounding_span": "ﬁne-tuning", "location": "§2"},
    {"id": "c-rlhf", "name": "RLHF", "aliases": ["reinforcement learning from human feedback"], "description": "Human-feedback approach to improve GAI system performance.", "grounding_span": "RLHF", "location": "§3 MS-2.5-002"},
    {"id": "c-model-distillation", "name": "model distillation or compression", "aliases": [], "description": "Methods creating smaller model versions that could reduce inference-time environmental impact.", "grounding_span": "model distillation or compression", "location": "§2.5"},
    {"id": "c-synthetic-data", "name": "synthetic data", "aliases": [], "description": "Model-generated data usable for development but risking homogenization and model collapse.", "grounding_span": "synthetic data", "location": "Appendix A.1.6"},
    {"id": "c-ai-actors", "name": "AI Actors", "aliases": [], "description": "Those who play an active role in the AI system lifecycle, per OECD.", "grounding_span": "AI Actors", "location": "§3"},
    {"id": "c-risk-tolerance", "name": "organizational risk tolerance", "aliases": [], "description": "The level of risk an organization is willing to accept, guiding controls and go/no-go decisions.", "grounding_span": "organizational risk tolerance", "location": "§3 MG-3.1-001"},
    {"id": "c-xai", "name": "explainable AI", "aliases": ["XAI"], "description": "Techniques (embeddings analysis, gradient attributions, occlusion) mitigating unexplainable GAI risks.", "grounding_span": "explainable AI (XAI) techniques", "location": "§3 MG-3.2-001"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d-gai", "term": "Generative AI", "verbatim_text": "the class of AI models that emulate the structure and characteristics of input data in order to generate derived synthetic content. This can include images, videos, audio, text, and other digital content.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023", "grounding_span": "the class of AI models that emulate the structure and characteristics of input", "location": "§1 footnote 1"},
    {"id": "d-dual-use-fm", "term": "dual-use foundation models", "verbatim_text": "an AI model that is trained on broad data; generally uses self-supervision; contains at least tens of billions of parameters; is applicable across a wide range of contexts.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023", "grounding_span": "an AI model that is trained on broad data; generally uses self-supervision; contains at least tens of", "location": "§1 footnote 1"},
    {"id": "d-confabulation", "term": "Confabulation", "verbatim_text": "a phenomenon in which GAI systems generate and confidently present erroneous or false content in response to prompts.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "refers to a phenomenon in which GAI systems generate and conﬁdently present", "location": "§2.2"},
    {"id": "d-risk", "term": "risk", "verbatim_text": "the composite measure of an event's probability (or likelihood) of occurring and the magnitude or degree of the consequences of the corresponding event.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "risk refers to the composite measure of an event’s probability", "location": "§2"},
    {"id": "d-info-integrity", "term": "information integrity", "verbatim_text": "spectrum of information and associated patterns of its creation, exchange, and consumption in society.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2022", "grounding_span": "spectrum of information and associated patterns of its creation,", "location": "§2.8"},
    {"id": "d-ai-actors", "term": "AI Actors", "verbatim_text": "those who play an active role in the AI system lifecycle, including organizations and individuals that deploy or operate AI.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2023", "grounding_span": "those who play an active role in the AI system lifecycle, including", "location": "§3 footnote 14"},
    {"id": "d-profile", "term": "profile", "verbatim_text": "an implementation of the AI RMF functions, categories, and subcategories for a specific setting, application, or technology.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "A proﬁle is an implementation of the AI RMF functions, categories, and subcategories for a speciﬁc", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "d-ai-incident", "term": "AI incidents", "verbatim_text": "event, circumstance, or series of events where the development, use, or malfunction of one or more AI systems directly or indirectly contributes to one of the following harms.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024", "grounding_span": "malfunction of one or more AI systems directly or indirectly contributes to one of the following harms:", "location": "Appendix A.1.8"},
    {"id": "d-red-teaming", "term": "AI Red-teaming", "verbatim_text": "A structured testing exercise used to probe an AI system to find flaws and vulnerabilities such as inaccurate, harmful, or discriminatory outputs, often in a controlled environment and in collaboration with system developers.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "vulnerabilities such as inaccurate, harmful, or discriminatory outputs, often in a controlled", "location": "Appendix A.1.5"},
    {"id": "d-algorithmic-monoculture", "term": "algorithmic monocultures", "verbatim_text": "the phenomenon in which repeated use of the same model or algorithm in consequential decision-making settings like employment and lending can result in increased susceptibility by systems to correlated failures (like unexpected shocks), due to multiple actors relying on the same algorithm.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "refers to the phenomenon in which repeated use of the same model or algorithm in", "location": "§2 footnote 3"},
    {"id": "d-jailbreaking", "term": "jailbreaking", "verbatim_text": "manipulating prompts to circumvent output controls.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "manipulating prompts to circumvent output controls.", "location": "§2.3"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl-carbon", "claim_text": "Training a single transformer LLM can emit as much carbon as 300 round-trip flights between San Francisco and New York.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "training a single transformer LLM can emit as much carbon as 300 round-", "location": "§2.5"},
    {"id": "cl-cbrn-minimal", "claim_text": "State-of-the-art LLMs at the time of study did not substantially increase the operational likelihood of a biological attack beyond traditional search.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "do not substantially increase the operational", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "cl-csam-datasets", "claim_text": "Several commonly used GAI training datasets were found to contain hundreds of known images of CSAM.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "several commonly used GAI training datasets were found to contain hundreds of known images of", "location": "§2.11"},
    {"id": "cl-pentagon", "claim_text": "A synthetic image of a Pentagon blast went viral and briefly caused a drop in the stock market.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Pentagon blast went viral and brieﬂy caused a drop in the stock market.", "location": "§2.8"},
    {"id": "cl-t2i-bias", "claim_text": "Current text-to-image models underrepresent women and/or racial minorities and people with disabilities.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "text-to-image models underrepresent women and/or racial minorities, and people with disabilities.", "location": "§2.6"},
    {"id": "cl-legal-confab", "claim_text": "Legal confabulations have been shown to be pervasive in current state-of-the-art LLMs.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "legal confabulations have been shown to be pervasive in current state-of-the-art LLMs.", "location": "§2.2 footnote 10"},
    {"id": "cl-vuln-discovery", "claim_text": "LLMs are already able to discover some vulnerabilities in systems and write code to exploit them.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "vulnerabilities in systems (hardware, software, data) and write code to exploit them.", "location": "§2.9"},
    {"id": "cl-apply-rmf", "claim_text": "Organizations should apply the AI RMF and Playbook activities when managing GAI system risk.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "apply the activities suggested in the AI RMF and its Playbook when managing the risk of GAI systems.", "location": "§3"},
    {"id": "cl-no-formal-channels", "claim_text": "Formal channels do not currently exist to report and document AI incidents.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Formal channels do not currently exist to report and document AI incidents.", "location": "Appendix A.1.8"},
    {"id": "cl-no-env-method", "claim_text": "There is currently no agreed-upon method to estimate environmental impacts from GAI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Currently there is no agreed upon method to estimate", "location": "§2.5"},
    {"id": "cl-cbrn-future", "claim_text": "In the future, GAI may enable malicious actors to more easily access CBRN weapons and relevant knowledge.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "malicious actors to more easily access CBRN weapons", "location": "§2.1"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "i-dioptra", "name": "NIST Dioptra", "owner": "NIST", "year": null, "method": "purpose-built testing environment for empirical evaluation of GAI trustworthy characteristics", "grounding_span": "a purpose-built testing environment such as NIST Dioptra", "location": "§3 MS-2.3-004"},
    {"id": "i-winogender", "name": "Winogender Schemas", "owner": null, "year": null, "method": "paired-sentence gender bias evaluation for coreference resolution", "grounding_span": "Winogender Schemas is a sample set of paired sentences which diﬀer only by gender of the pronouns used", "location": "§3 footnote 15"},
    {"id": "i-bbq", "name": "Bias Benchmark Questions", "owner": null, "year": null, "method": "benchmark quantifying systemic bias in GAI outputs", "grounding_span": "Bias Benchmark Questions", "location": "§3 MS-2.11-001"},
    {"id": "i-rhhp", "name": "Real Hateful or Harmful Prompts", "owner": null, "year": null, "method": "benchmark for stereotyping, denigration, and hateful content", "grounding_span": "Real Hateful or Harmful Prompts", "location": "§3 MS-2.11-001"}
  ],
  "measures": [
    {"id": "m-winogender-pairs", "text": "paired sentences which differ only by gender of the pronouns used", "response_type": "paired comparison", "grounding_span": "paired sentences which diﬀer only by gender of the pronouns used", "location": "§3 footnote 15"}
  ],
  "standards": [
    {"id": "s-sbom", "name": "software bills of materials", "version": null, "steward": null, "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "software bills of materials (SBOMs)", "location": "Appendix A.1.3"},
    {"id": "s-sla", "name": "service level agreements", "version": null, "steward": null, "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "service level agreements (SLAs)", "location": "§3 GV-6.1-004"},
    {"id": "s-ssae", "name": "statement on standards for attestation engagement reports", "version": null, "steward": null, "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "attestation engagement (SSAE) reports", "location": "Appendix A.1.3"},
    {"id": "s-ai-incident-db", "name": "AI incident databases (AVID, CVE, NVD, OECD AI incident monitor)", "version": null, "steward": null, "as_of_date": "2024-07", "grounding_span": "AVID, CVE, NVD, or OECD AI incident monitor", "location": "§3 GV-1.6-003"}
  ],
  "frameworks": [

    {"grounding_span": "Tags linking suggested actions with relevant GAI risks.", "suggested_edge": "suggested_action_mitigates_risk", "note": "The 'GAI Risks' column links each suggested action to the risks it manages; suggested actions are not a node type and 'mitigates' is not an allowed edge.", "location": "§3"},
